# Unused `_raw_<X>` Exposures Triage

*Auto-generated by `scripts/find_unused_raw_exposures.py` (br-r37-c1-fmggo).*

Total `_raw_*` exposures with only the import-line reference: **47**

## Triage decisions

Three categories:

- **keep-public-api**: the wrapper deliberately takes a different path (byte-identical nx tie-break, type-coerce post-process, ebunch handling, etc.) but the `_raw_<X>` is kept as the direct-Rust API surface.
- **wire-up**: a wrapper currently delegates to nx even though `_raw_<X>` exists — perf win opportunity if wired up.
- **remove**: no rationale to keep; candidate for deletion.

## keep-public-api (47)

| binding | rationale |
|---------|-----------|
| `_raw_adamic_adar_index` | wrapper handles ebunch=None case via Python iterator |
| `_raw_antichains` | wrapper accepts topo_order kwarg the binding lacks |
| `_raw_average_node_connectivity` | br-r37-c1-qz40o: Rust BFS-based local_node_connectivity is a heuristic that mis-reports in non-trivial cases; wrapper delegates to nx's max-flow-on-auxiliary-graph path.  Raw kept as direct API. |
| `_raw_bellman_ford_path` | br-r37-c1-9axrp: Rust bellman_ford_path returns incorrect paths (direct edge instead of shorter multi-hop); wrapper delegates to nx until the Rust binding is fixed.  Raw kept as direct API. |
| `_raw_bellman_ford_path_length` | br-r37-c1-9axrp: same Rust bug as _raw_bellman_ford_path; wrapper delegates to nx.  Raw kept as direct API. |
| `_raw_biconnected_component_edges` | wrapper produces edge-tuple format from raw component output |
| `_raw_clique_removal` | br-r37-c1-xgqo1: nx.approximation.clique_removal routes fnx.approximation.X; raw kept as direct-Rust API |
| `_raw_common_neighbors` | wrapper materializes neighbors via G.adj for nx parity |
| `_raw_compose` | wrapper handles MultiGraph/MultiDiGraph dispatch |
| `_raw_dag_longest_path` | wrapper handles weight callable and topological_sort kwarg |
| `_raw_dag_longest_path_length` | wrapper has int/float type-coerce post-process (br-r37-c1-oqspv) |
| `_raw_degree_histogram` | wrapper handles MultiGraph parallel-edge counting |
| `_raw_efficiency` | wrapper has u/v node-existence pre-check |
| `_raw_eulerian_circuit` | wrapper has source-node selection and order normalization |
| `_raw_find_cliques` | wrapper handles nodes-subset kwarg and iteration order |
| `_raw_find_cliques_recursive` | exposed for direct callers; public uses iterative variant |
| `_raw_floyd_warshall` | wrapper delegates to nx for iteration-order parity (br-r37-c1-h1kf2); raw kept as direct-Rust API |
| `_raw_floyd_warshall_predecessor_and_distance` | wrapper delegates to nx for iteration-order parity (br-r37-c1-h1kf2) |
| `_raw_global_node_connectivity` | br-r37-c1-ccr8k: fnx-only top-level removed; raw kept for direct callers (use node_connectivity for nx parity) |
| `_raw_greedy_modularity_communities` | br-r37-c1-uwm5v: fnx.community.X falls through to nx; raw kept as direct-Rust API |
| `_raw_intersection` | wrapper handles MultiGraph/MultiDiGraph dispatch |
| `_raw_is_aperiodic` | wrapper does emptyish-graph short-circuit before Rust call |
| `_raw_is_attracting_component` | wrapper accepts a node, raw works on components — different shape |
| `_raw_is_isolate` | wrapper checks G.is_directed() to dispatch correctly |
| `_raw_jaccard_coefficient` | wrapper handles ebunch=None case via Python iterator |
| `_raw_kosaraju_strongly_connected_components` | wrapper uses Tarjan-style native path; Kosaraju kept as alternative |
| `_raw_large_clique_size` | br-r37-c1-dytcs: fnx.approximation.large_clique_size falls through to nx; raw kept as direct-Rust API |
| `_raw_lexicographic_topological_sort` | called from lexicographic_topological_sort wrapper after preflight |
| `_raw_louvain_communities` | br-r37-c1-uwm5v: fnx.community.louvain_communities falls through to nx (with fg->ng conversion); raw kept as direct-Rust API |
| `_raw_max_clique` | br-r37-c1-xgqo1: fnx.approximation.max_clique falls through to nx; raw kept as direct-Rust API |
| `_raw_max_weight_clique` | br-r37-c1-07gkp: Rust max_weight_clique returns clique *size* instead of summing node weights, and rejects weight=None (br-mwclqnone). Wrapper delegates to nx; raw kept as direct API. |
| `_raw_max_weight_matching` | exposed for direct callers; public wrapper has additional tuple-direction parity logic (br-r37-c1-edgekeyfirstwins / matching) |
| `_raw_maximum_independent_set` | br-r37-c1-xgqo1: fnx.approximation.maximum_independent_set falls through to nx; raw kept as direct-Rust API |
| `_raw_min_edge_cover` | exposed for direct callers; public wrapper has nx-parity post-process |
| `_raw_min_weight_matching` | same rationale as _raw_max_weight_matching |
| `_raw_min_weighted_vertex_cover` | br-r37-c1-xgqo1: fnx.approximation.min_weighted_vertex_cover falls through to nx; raw kept as direct-Rust API |
| `_raw_minimum_node_cut` | wrapper has additional flow-based handling for s/t cases |
| `_raw_non_edges` | wrapper has DiGraph special-case for direction |
| `_raw_preferential_attachment` | wrapper handles ebunch=None case via Python iterator |
| `_raw_random_spanning_tree` | wrapper has seed/multiplicity handling layer |
| `_raw_resource_allocation_index` | wrapper handles ebunch=None case via Python iterator |
| `_raw_shortest_simple_paths` | wrapper has Yen's-algorithm Python orchestration; raw kernel is BFS-based primitive |
| `_raw_single_source_dijkstra_path` | wrapper composes from _raw_single_source_dijkstra (yields paths alongside dists in one pass) |
| `_raw_single_source_dijkstra_path_length` | wrapper composes from _raw_single_source_dijkstra (more general — yields dists+paths in one pass) |
| `_raw_topological_sort` | wrapper re-implements Kahn's in Python for byte-identical nx tie-break order (br-codtrav) |
| `_raw_transitive_reduction` | exposed for direct DAG callers; public wrapper has DiGraph-only check |
| `_raw_voterank` | wrapper has number_of_results / max_iter handling |
